profile.name: Spatial Analysis Agent
profile.description: End-to-end LLM-driven geoprocessing pipeline. Audits input datasets to build a data registry, asks an LLM to design a NetworkX workflow DAG that solves the task, generates Python code for each operation node, assembles the operations into a single executable program, and runs it in a sandboxed environment. A pre-built workflow graph (GraphML) and/or pre-built data registry may be supplied as parameters to skip the corresponding stage.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: End-to-End Spatial Analysis Workflow
skills.description: From a natural-language task and a set of input datasets, the agent: (1) audits each dataset to build a data registry (shape, columns, dtypes, head sample); (2) asks an LLM to design a NetworkX workflow DAG with parallel branches as appropriate; (3) generates Python code for each operation node with ancestor/descendant context; (4) assembles all operations into one executable program and runs it in a sandbox. On runtime failure, an LLM repair pass is attempted, followed by a regen-from-scratch fallback. The workflow's final output node(s) — the result the user needs, of whatever file type fits — are written to a per-task OUTPUT_DIR, discovered after execution, and reported as artifacts; intermediates are kept in memory.

constraints.spatial: Operates on whichever spatial data the graph references; CRS handling is the responsibility of the generated operation code.
other: If a pre-built GraphML is supplied via parameters.workflow_graph_path (or as a .graphml/.gml entry in input_datasets), the workflow generation stage is skipped.
If a pre-built data registry is supplied via parameters.data_registry, the audit stage is skipped.

Only the workflow's final output node(s) are written to the per-task OUTPUT_DIR and discovered after execution; intermediate results are passed between functions in memory and are not required on disk.
Assembly code is executed via exec() in a sandboxed working directory; on failure, a single LLM repair pass and a single regen-from-scratch fallback are attempted.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language geospatial task together with a workflow graph to the agent. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution.

instructions.description: Natural-language description of the analysis question the workflow graph is meant to answer.

input_datasets.description: Data files to be audited and consumed by the workflow (e.g., zipped shapefiles, GeoPackages, GeoJSON, CSVs, rasters). A pre-built workflow graph (.graphml) may also be included; if present, it is used as-is instead of the LLM-generated one.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response.

primary_artifacts.description: The workflow's final output node(s), written to the per-task OUTPUT_DIR by the executed assembly program. The format is whatever fits the result and is not limited to the formats listed here.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model. Stage-specific graph_model, operation_model, and assembly_model parameters can override individual stages.

workflow_graph_path.description: Explicit path to a pre-built GraphML workflow file. When provided, the workflow generation stage is skipped and this graph is used directly.

data_registry.description: Optional list of {file, path, shape, columns, head, source} objects describing the available input datasets. When provided, the dataset audit stage is skipped.

workflow_code.description: Optional Python code that built the workflow graph. Provided to the LLM as additional context when generating operation code.

graph_model.description: Model used to generate the workflow graph design code.

operation_model.description: Model used to generate code for each individual operation node.

assembly_model.description: Model used to assemble all operation functions into a single executable program (also used for repair / regeneration).

credentials.description: Provide one model execution credential in the request credentials object.

provenance.description: Reports per-operation lineage (node_name + description), llm_calls, code execution count, and token usage.

reproducibility.description: The assembled Python program executed for the task is returned in the script field and persisted to disk alongside its created artifacts.

validation.description: The agent does not currently perform domain-level validation of the generated workflow; downstream review is recommended.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile.

operation_policy.description: Generated code is executed via exec() in a per-task working directory. Outputs are restricted by convention to a single OUTPUT_DIR.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the relevant research on geospatial AI agents.

extensions.description: This agent ports the SpatialAnalysisExecutor pattern: it converts a NetworkX workflow DAG into a runnable Python program via LLM code generation per operation node, then a single LLM assembly pass, then sandboxed execution.
